markdown:
Audit complete. Created inbox item I-0017 (info, to=ground, agent=diff-auditor). The run-0004 T1 merge is clean on all three axes.

Findings:

1. PullbackTensorComp.lean retirement is clean. The file is deleted and its root import removed from `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` (only a descriptive comment remains). The absorbed D3′ decls each resolve to exactly one definition in `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` — no duplicate fully-qualified decl across two root-imported modules, so no fatal clash:
   - `pullbackTensorMap_restrict` (L3312), `sheafificationCompPullback_comp` (L2554), `toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile` (L2141), `sheaf_unit_comp_pushforward_pullbackComp_inv` (L2494), `pullbackValIso_comp`→`pullbackValIso_comp_leg` (L3180).
   - The two helpers that were NOT re-homed (`pullbackValIso_eq_sheafCompPb`, `sheafCompPb_counit_comp_coherence`) are referenced nowhere outside the deleted file — genuinely dead, safe to drop. Consumers in `TensorObjInverse.lean` / `DualInverse/PresheafDualPullback.lean` / `PullbackTensorIso.lean` all resolve to the single `TensorObjSubstrate` definitions.

2. Zero buried sorries. A comment-and-backtick-aware scan of every merge-cone file found no `sorry`/`admit` in code position — every hit is comment/docstring prose. No new `axiom`/`native_decide`/`implemented_by`/`opaque` introduced. The keystone `Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse` (`/AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean:3312`) is a genuine sorry-free gluing proof with the correct, unweakened signature (hyp `IsLocallyTrivial L`; concl the L ⊗ L⁻¹ ≅ 𝒪_C keystone), closing with `exact ⟨asIso ε⟩`. Stale "PARTIAL/blocker" narration in its docstring is just outdated prose — `trivialisation_restrict_compat` is actually consumed as a proved lemma.

3. No T1 stray files. Attribution confirmed: `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImageUnconditional.lean` (pre-existing root import, edited, outside the Picard merge-cone) and the root `scratch_bareBC.lean` / `scratch_probe.lean` are the concurrent run-0003 T2 session's in-flight work swept into the checkpoint — `scratch_bareBC.lean` and the Cech file are still dirty (`M`) in the worktree after the commit, evidence of a separate live writer, not T1. The +10332-line `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` change is the expected wholesale blueprint lift/reconciliation for the merged substrate.

No action needed; no source files edited (build deliberately not run, per the T2-lock constraint).

Note: the inbox CLI emitted an unrelated warning about a mathlib pin mismatch (`v4.30.0` vs declared `v4.31.0`) in `SubProjects/RelatedPapersFormalisation/MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard/lake-manifest.json` — outside my audit scope, flagging in passing.
